findings.finding: CHANGELOG.md 0.5.0 describes round-aware file conventions and `action`-field routing as live features, but check_convergence.py has not been updated to implement them (Phases 1-4 not shipped). A user reading the CHANGELOG today would believe round-aware loading, the `action` field, `evaluate_stage5_dual`, and the removal of `evaluate_stage5_two_tier` are all live. They are not.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Known gaps / In progress' subsection to CHANGELOG.md 0.5.0 noting: '`check_convergence.py` update (round-aware loaders + `action` field + `evaluate_stage5_dual`): Phase 0 characterization tests shipped; Phases 1-4 implementation pending. Tracked in `specs/check-convergence-round-aware/README.md`.'

findings.finding: No STATUS.md exists in the spec directory. The master plan references STATUS.md as the landing zone for deferred follow-ups DF1 (marketplace cache refresh) and DF2 (duplicate check_convergence.py cleanup). Stage 7 (live verification + STATUS.md) was not run. The deferred items have no formal tracking artifact.
findings.suggested_fix: Create a minimal `specs/check-convergence-round-aware/STATUS.md` listing DF1 and DF2 as open deferred items with owner and expected-by date, or add them to a project-level follow-up tracker.

findings.finding: reviewer-convergence.md Section 3 body still describes 0.4.0 flat-path loading ('Collect all <role>_review.json files') and 0.4.0 Stage 5 tier-1/tier-2 semantics. Phase 3 is spec'd to fix this, but there is no inline annotation indicating the section is intentionally stale pending that phase. A cold reader of Section 3 is misled about current behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an HTML comment at the top of Section 3: '<!-- NOTE: Section 3 body describes 0.4.0 flat-path conventions. Will be updated to round-aware paths in Phase 3 of check-convergence-round-aware. -->'
findings.notes: Section 0 and Section 4 of reviewer-convergence.md already correctly document 0.5.0 semantics. Only Section 3 body is stale.

findings.finding: TDD plan table Phase 3 row still reads 'deprecation warning' in the 'What they verify' column. Five of six stale locations were fixed; this cell was missed. The Phase 3 spec section above the table correctly says 'REMOVE / ImportError'. Flagged by round-3 architect-reviewer (N2) and round-3 tester (NEW-1) and not applied before Phase 0 commit.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the cell text from 'evaluate_stage5_dual single-shot semantics; deprecation warning' to 'evaluate_stage5_dual single-shot semantics; removal of evaluate_stage5_two_tier (ImportError test)'.

closed_findings.note: Round-3 A6-partial and T6 flagged test_integration_stage3_round_2_with_carryover_escalate description contradiction at line 417. Current README reads 'iteration=4, max_iterations=3' with explicit boundary explanation — consistent with the _escalate test name. Fix was applied before Phase 0 commit. Confirmed closed on current HEAD.

cross_phase_assessment.notes: Phase 0 is clean. The codebase is not in an inconsistent state. check_convergence.py is unchanged; 18 characterization tests pass; rollback is trivial. Documentation gaps (H1, M1, M2, L1) are the only issues. No code changes required — all fixes are documentation-only.

dogfood_assessment.notes: The 0.5.0 skill produced all expected Stage 1-5 artifacts for Phase 0. The 3-round plan review surfaced real findings (cap asymmetry bug, 5 security defenses, shim-vs-removal decision). Phase-end tester independently re-ran the suite and cross-checked evidence. Stage 5 is firing now. The one gap: DF3 (subagent file-write propagation in running sessions) was noted in phase-0-state.md and affects the dogfood fidelity but not the production correctness of Phase 0.
